NFP | Intraday post (11/Feb)

Weekly post:

Python course is launched | Weekly post (09-13/Feb)

I’m happy to announce that the Python course is now available on my website for all paid subscribers. See details by clicking on the link.

For Tuesday, the weekly post predicted benign ranging as it happened and kept the structure, however, without trend being built. I say this since December. For today, the weekly post also predicts benign price action, with forced positiveness no matter what happens. Volatility and anxiety remains high, but suppressive forces are expected to kick in tomorrow.

NFPNonfarm payrolls (NFP): +130k in January. Unemployment rate: 4.3%Private payrolls: +172k implying that government was a drag on the headline.Sector mix: gains concentrated in health care (+82k), social assistance (+42k), construction (+33k); losses in federal government (-34k) and financial activities (-22k). As hiring didn’t roll over in January; it’s not a recession-style print. I talked about it. However, also 0.4% m/m AHE is not cold. It hints idea that labor-cost disinflation isn’t guaranteed. But not tragic. Soft-landing narrative is alive.

Let’s look at the daily positioning…

INTRADAY POST Coding today’s positioning:

Speed profile is flat to negative. Today customers are selling downside vol quite confidently, and also upside vol too. Vvol is sold into both ways. Customers are expecting momentum, mostly in above the centroid.

Centroid is at 6946.74 - 6951.08; today it acts locally as a very important structural pivot too, with reversion opportunities at its lower band, and some weak stickiness at the upper bad.

Upside pivot is at 7011.70with target of 7021.72This is most likely a rejection band, with local pin around 6995.

Downside pivot is at 6895.29with target of 6882.76This is a very sticky and supportive area especially during the PM session.

So, as long as the spot is above the centroid (and it will very likely stay there), there are magnetic forces upward. Market is still betting on ranging, but with a higher volume move now. Most likely some reversion trades, as structurally both the SPX and ES are weak.Still keep your eyes on ES 6960/70 zone as the key pivot. It will give a signal earlier than SPX, or anything now. The fact that it could not conquer 7005/10, is very telling.As the weekly post said: no trend, but some local support into Wednesday.
